fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: BRTHDAT
fields.label: Birth Date
fields.questionText: What is the subject's date of birth?
fields.definition: A subject's date of birth (with or without the time of birth). The complete Date of Birth is made from the temporal components of Birth Year, Birth Month, Birth Day, and Birth Time..

fields.name: BRTHTIM
fields.label: Birth Time
fields.questionText: What is the subject's time of birth?
fields.definition: The time of birth of the subject, in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm).

fields.name: AGE
fields.label: Age
fields.questionText: What is the subject's age?
fields.definition: The age of the subject, expressed in AGEU.

fields.name: AGEU
fields.label: Age Units
fields.questionText: What is the age unit used?
fields.definition: Units of time that are routinely used to express the age of a person

fields.name: DMDAT
fields.label: Demographics Collection Date
fields.questionText: What is the date of collection?
fields.definition: The date of collection, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: SEX
fields.label: Sex
fields.questionText: What is the sex of the subject?
fields.definition: Sex of the subject, as determined by the investigator.

fields.name: ETHNIC
fields.label: Ethnicity
fields.questionText: Do you consider yourself Hispanic/Latino or not Hispanic/Latino?
fields.definition: A social group characterized by a distinctive social and cultural tradition maintained from generation to generation, a common history and origin and a sense of identification with the group; members of the group have distinctive features in their way of life, shared experiences and often a common g

fields.name: CETHNIC
fields.label: Collected Ethnicity
fields.questionText: What is the ethnicity of the subject?
fields.definition: A social group characterized by a distinctive social and cultural tradition maintained from generation to generation, a common history and origin and a sense of identification with the group; members of the group have distinctive features in their way of life, shared experiences, and often a common

fields.name: RACE
fields.label: Race
fields.questionText: Which of the following five racial designations best describes you? (More than one choice is acceptable.)
fields.definition: An arbitrary classification based on physical characteristics; a group of persons related by common descent or heredity (US Center for Disease Control).

fields.name: CRACE
fields.label: Collected Race
fields.questionText: Which of the following racial designations best describes you? (More than one choice is acceptable.)
fields.definition: An arbitrary classification based on physical characteristics; a group of persons related by common descent or heredity (US Centers for Disease Control).

fields.name: RACEOTH
fields.label: Race Other
fields.questionText: What was the other race?
fields.definition: A free-text field to be used when none of the preprinted values for RACE are applicable or if another, unprinted selection should be added to those preprinted values.

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: VISIT
fields.label: Visit Name
fields.questionText: What is the visit name?
fields.definition: The name of a clinical encounter that encompasses planned and unplanned trial interventions, procedures, and assessments that may be performed on a subject.

fields.name: VISDAT
fields.label: Visit Date
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the date of the visit?
fields.definition: Date the clinical encounter occurred (or started).

fields.name: IEYN
fields.label: Any Incl/Excl Criteria Findings
fields.questionText: Were all eligibility criteria met?
fields.definition: Indication whether the subject met all the eligibility requirements for this study at the time the subject was enrolled.

fields.name: IEDAT
fields.label: Inclusion/Exclusion Collection Date
fields.questionText: What was the date the eligibility criteria assessment was performed?
fields.definition: The date of collection of the inclusion/exclusion criteria represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: IECAT
fields.label: Inclusion/Exclusion Category
fields.questionText: What was the category of the criterion?
fields.definition: A grouping category to denote whether the protocol entry criterion being assessed is inclusion criteria or exclusion criteria.

fields.name: IESCAT
fields.label: Inclusion/Exclusion Subcategory
fields.questionText: What was the subcategory of the criterion?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the IECAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: IETESTCD
fields.label: Inclusion/Exclusion Criterion Short Name
fields.questionText: What was the identifier of the inclusion criterion the subject did not meet or the exclusion criterion the subject met?
fields.definition: The unique identifier associated with the criterion that was the exception.

fields.name: IETEST
fields.label: Inclusion/Exclusion Criterion
fields.questionText: What was the description of the inclusion criterion the subject did not meet or the exclusion criterion the subject met?
fields.definition: Descriptive name of the inclusion or exclusion criterion that was the exception.

fields.name: IEORRES
fields.label: I/E Criterion Original Result
fields.questionText: What is the result?
fields.definition: An indication of which inclusion criterion was not met or exclusion criterion was met.

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: MHYN
fields.label: Any Medical History Event
fields.questionText: Were any medical conditions or events reported?; Has the subject had any medical conditions or events?
fields.definition: An indication of whether there was any medical history to report.

fields.name: MHCAT
fields.label: Category for Medical History
fields.questionText: What was the category of the medical history?
fields.definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: MHSCAT
fields.label: Subcategory for Medical History
fields.questionText: What was the subcategory of the medical history?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the MHCAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: MHDAT
fields.label: Medical History Collection Date
fields.questionText: What was the date the medical history was collected?
fields.definition: The date on which the medical history was collected, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: MHSPID
fields.label: MH Sponsor-Defined Identifier
fields.questionText: [Sponsor-defined question]
fields.definition: A sponsor-defined identifier. In CDASH, this is typically used for preprinted or auto-generated numbers on the CRF, or any other type of identifier that does not already have a defined CDASH identifier field.

fields.name: MHEVDTYP
fields.label: Medical History Event Date Type
fields.questionText: What was the medical history event date type?
fields.definition: Specifies the aspect of the medical condition or event by which MHSTDTC and/or MHENDTC is defined.

fields.name: MHTERM
fields.label: Reported Term for the Medical History
fields.questionText: What is the medical condition or event term?
fields.definition: The reported or prespecified name of the medical condition or event.

fields.name: MHOCCUR
fields.label: Medical History Occurrence
fields.questionText: Did the subject have [prespecified medical condition/event/group of medical conditions]; Is the [prespecified medical occurring]?
fields.definition: An indication of whether a prespecified medical condition/event or a group of medical conditions/events occurred when information about the occurrence of a specific event is solicited.

fields.name: MHPRESP
fields.label: Medical History Event Prespecified
fields.definition: An indication that a specific event, or group of events, are prespecified on a CRF.

fields.name: MHPRIOR
fields.label: Prior Medical History Event
fields.questionText: Did the medical condition or event start prior to [MHSTTPT]?; Did the medical condition or event start prior to study start?
fields.definition: An indication of whether the event occurred prior to study start.

fields.name: MHONGO
fields.label: Ongoing Medical History Event
fields.prompt: Ongoing (as of the [study-specific timepoint or period])
fields.questionText: Is the medical condition or event ongoing (as of the [study-specific timepoint or period])?
fields.definition: Indication the medical condition or event is ongoing when no end date is provided.

fields.name: MHCTRL
fields.label: MH Disease or Symptom Under Control
fields.questionText: Is the medical condition or event under control?
fields.definition: Indication of whether the medical condition or event is under control at the time of data collection.

fields.name: MHSTDAT
fields.label: Medical History Event Start Date
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the [medical event or condition/category of the event] start date?
fields.definition: The start date of medical history event or condition, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: MHENDAT
fields.label: Medical History Event End Date
fields.questionText: What[is/was] the[medical event or condition/category of the event] end date?
fields.definition: The end date of medical history event or condition, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: MHLOC
fields.label: Medical History Event Location
fields.questionText: What was the anatomical location of the medical condition or event?
fields.definition: A description of the anatomical location relevant for the medical condition or event.

fields.name: MHLAT
fields.label: Medical History Event Laterality
fields.questionText: What was the side of the anatomical location of the medical condition or event?
fields.definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing the side of the body relevant for the event.

fields.name: MHDIR
fields.label: Medical History Directionality
fields.questionText: What was the directionality of the anatomical location of the medical condition or event?
fields.definition: Qualifier further detailing the position of the anatomical location, relative to the center of the body, organ, or specimen.

fields.name: MHPORTOT
fields.label: MH Event Location Portion or Totality
fields.questionText: What was the portion or totality of the anatomical location of the of the medical condition or event?
fields.definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing the distribution (i.e., arrangement of, apportioning of).

fields.name: MHMODIFY
fields.label: MH Modified Reported Term
fields.definition: If the value for MHTERM is modified to facilitate coding, then MHMODIFY will contain the modified text.

fields.name: MHDECOD
fields.label: MH Dictionary-Derived Term
fields.definition: The dictionary text description of MHTERM or the modified topic variable (MH MODIFY), if applicable.

fields.name: MHLLT
fields.label: Medical History Event Lowest Level Term
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the lowest level term.

fields.name: MHLLTCD
fields.label: MH Event Lowest Level Term Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the lowest level term.

fields.name: MHPTCD
fields.label: MH Event Preferred Term Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the preferred term.

fields.name: MHHLT
fields.label: Medical History Event High Level Term
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the high level term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: MHHLTCD
fields.label: MH Event High Level Term Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the high level term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: MHHLGT
fields.label: MH Event High Level Group Term
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the high level group term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: MHHLGTCD
fields.label: MH Event High Level Group Term Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the high level group term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: MHSOC
fields.label: MH Event Primary System Organ Class
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: MHSOCCD
fields.label: MH Event Primary System Organ Class Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What is the subject identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: VISIT
fields.label: Visit Name
fields.questionText: What is the visit name?
fields.definition: The name of a clinical encounter that encompasses planned and unplanned trial interventions, procedures, and assessments that may be performed on a subject.

fields.name: VISDAT
fields.label: Visit Date
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the date of the visit?
fields.definition: Date the clinical encounter occurred (or started).

fields.name: VSPERF
fields.label: Vital Signs Performed
fields.questionText: Were vital signs performed?
fields.definition: An indication of whether a planned vital signs measurement, series of vital signs measurements, tests, or observations was performed.

fields.name: VSDAT
fields.label: Vital Signs Date
fields.questionText: What was the date of the vital signs measurement?
fields.definition: The date of the vital signs measurement, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: VSTIM
fields.label: Vital Signs Time
fields.questionText: What was the time of the vital signs measurement?
fields.definition: The time of measurement, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: VSSPID
fields.label: Vital Signs Sponsor-Defined Identifier
fields.questionText: [Sponsor-defined question]
fields.definition: A sponsor-defined identifier. In CDASH, this is typically used for preprinted or auto-generated numbers on the CRF, or any other type of identifier that does not already have a defined CDASH identifier field.

fields.name: VSTPT
fields.label: Vital Signs Planned Time Point Name
fields.questionText: What is the planned time point for this vital signs measurement?
fields.definition: A text description of planned time points when measurements should be taken, as defined in the protocol.

fields.name: VSCAT
fields.label: Category for Vital Signs
fields.questionText: What was the category of the vital signs?
fields.definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: VSSCAT
fields.label: Subcategory for Vital Signs
fields.questionText: What was the subcategory of the vital signs?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the VSCAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: VSREPNUM
fields.label: Vital Signs Repetition Number
fields.questionText: What was the repetition number within the time point for this measurement?
fields.definition: The instance number of a test that is repeated within a given timeframe for the same test. The level of granularity can vary (e.g., within a time point, within a visit).

fields.name: VSTEST
fields.label: Vital Signs Test Name
fields.questionText: What is the vital sign test name?
fields.definition: Descriptive name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding.

fields.name: VSSTAT
fields.label: Vital Signs Completion Status
fields.questionText: Indicate if the vital signs measurement was not done
fields.definition: This variable is used to indicate that data are not available, by having the site recording the value as "Not Done".

fields.name: VSORRES
fields.label: VS Result or Finding in Original Units
fields.questionText: What was the result of the measurement?
fields.definition: Result of the vital signs measurement as originally received or collected.

fields.name: VSORRESU
fields.label: VS Original Units
fields.questionText: What was the unit of the measurement?
fields.definition: The unit of the result as originally received or collected.

fields.name: VSCLSIG
fields.label: Vital Signs Clinical Significance
fields.questionText: Was the result clinically significant?
fields.definition: An indication whether the vital sign result was clinically significant.

fields.name: VSLOC
fields.label: Location of Vital Signs Measurement
fields.questionText: What was the anatomical location where the measurement was taken?
fields.definition: A description of the anatomical location of the subject, relevant to the collection of vital signs measurements.

fields.name: VSPOS
fields.label: Vital Signs Position of Subject
fields.questionText: What was the position of the subject during the measurement?
fields.definition: The position of the subject during a measurement or examination.

fields.name: VSDIR
fields.label: Vital Signs Directionality
fields.questionText: What was the directionality of the anatomical location of the measurement?
fields.definition: Qualifier further detailing the position of the anatomical location, relative to the center of the body, organ, or specimen.

fields.name: VSLAT
fields.label: Vital Signs Laterality
fields.questionText: What was the side of the anatomical location of the vital signs measurement?
fields.definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing the side of the body.

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: VISIT
fields.label: Visit Name
fields.questionText: What is the visit name?
fields.definition: The name of a clinical encounter that encompasses planned and unplanned trial interventions, procedures, and assessments that may be performed on a subject.

fields.name: VISDAT
fields.label: Visit Date
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the date of the visit?
fields.definition: Date the clinical encounter occurred (or started).

fields.name: EGCAT
fields.label: Category for ECG
fields.questionText: What was the category of the ECG finding?
fields.definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: EGSCAT
fields.label: Subcategory for ECG
fields.questionText: What was the subcategory of the ECG finding?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the EGCAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: EGPERF
fields.label: ECG Performed
fields.questionText: Was the ECG performed?
fields.definition: An indication of whether a planned ECG measurement, series of ECG measurements, tests, or observations was performed.

fields.name: EGREPNUM
fields.label: ECG Repetition Number
fields.questionText: Which repetition of the ECG is this?
fields.definition: The incidence number of a test that is repeated within a given timeframe for the same test. The level of granularity can vary (e.g., within a timepoint, within a visit; multiple measurements of blood pressure, multiple analyses of a sample).

fields.name: EGREFID
fields.label: ECG Reference ID
fields.questionText: What was the (ECG) [reference identifier/accession number]?
fields.definition: An internal or external identifier of the ECG (e.g., waveform number).

fields.name: EGMETHOD
fields.label: Method of ECG Test
fields.questionText: What was the method used for the ECG?
fields.definition: Method of the test or examination.

fields.name: EGLEAD
fields.label: ECG Lead Location Used for Measurement
fields.questionText: Which lead location was used for this measurement?
fields.definition: The lead used for the measurement (e.g., "LEAD I", "LEAD II", "LEAD III", "LEAD rV2", "LEAD V1")

fields.name: EGPOS
fields.label: ECG Position of Subject
fields.questionText: What was the position of the subject during the ECG measurement?
fields.definition: The position of the subject during the ECG measurement.

fields.name: EGDAT
fields.label: ECG Date
fields.questionText: What was the date of the ECG?
fields.definition: The date the ECG was performed, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: EGTPT
fields.label: ECG Planned Time Point Name
fields.questionText: What was the planned time point of the ECG measurement?
fields.definition: A text description of planned time point when measurements should be taken as defined in the protocol.

fields.name: EGTIM
fields.label: ECG Time
fields.questionText: What was the time the ECG was collected?
fields.definition: Time of ECG, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

sourceActivities.activityName: MMTT (glucose, insulin, C-peptide, GLP-1, GIP, glucagon, FFAs)

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: VISIT
fields.label: Visit Name
fields.questionText: What is the visit name?
fields.definition: The name of a clinical encounter that encompasses planned and unplanned trial interventions, procedures, and assessments that may be performed on a subject.

fields.name: VISDAT
fields.label: Visit Date
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the date of the visit?
fields.definition: Date the clinical encounter occurred (or started).

fields.name: LBPERF
fields.label: Lab Performed
fields.questionText: Was the sample collected?; Was the lab performed?
fields.definition: An indication of whether a planned lab measurement, series of lab measurements, tests, observations was performed or specimens collected.

fields.name: LBDAT
fields.label: Specimen Collection Date
fields.questionText: What was the (start) date of the lab specimen collection?
fields.definition: The date of specimen collection, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: LBTIM
fields.label: Specimen Collection Time
fields.questionText: What was the (start) time of the lab specimen collection?
fields.definition: The time of specimen collection, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: LBCAT
fields.label: Category for Lab Test
fields.questionText: What was the name of the lab panel?
fields.definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: LBSCAT
fields.label: Subcategory for Lab Test
fields.questionText: What was the name of the lab sub-panel?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the LBCAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: LBSPEC
fields.label: LB Specimen Type
fields.questionText: What is the specimen material type?
fields.definition: The type of sample material taken from a biological entity.

fields.name: LBTPT
fields.label: Lab Planned Time Point Name
fields.questionText: What was the planned time point of the lab?
fields.definition: A text description of planned time point when measurements should be taken, as defined in the protocol.

fields.name: LBCOND
fields.label: Lab Test Condition Met
fields.questionText: Were the protocol-defined testing conditions met?
fields.definition: Indication whether the testing conditions defined in the protocol were met (e.g., low-fat diet).

fields.name: LBFAST
fields.label: Lab Fasting Status
fields.questionText: Was the subject fasting?
fields.definition: An indication that the subject has abstained from food/water for the specified amount of time.

fields.name: LBREFID
fields.label: Lab Specimen ID
fields.questionText: What was the (laboratory test) [reference identifier/accession number]?
fields.definition: An internal or external identifier (e.g., specimen identifier).

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: EPOCH
fields.label: Epoch
fields.questionText: What is the trial epoch?
fields.definition: Name of the trial epoch with which this element of the arm is associated.

fields.name: EXYN
fields.label: Any Study Treatment Taken
fields.questionText: Were any[study treatment/dose] taken?
fields.definition: An indication of whether the subject took study medication/treatment.

fields.name: EXCAT
fields.label: Category of Treatment
fields.questionText: What is the category of the [study treatment/dose] ?
fields.definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: EXSCAT
fields.label: Subcategory of Treatment
fields.questionText: What is the subcategory of the [study treatment/dose] ?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the EXCAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: EXTRT
fields.label: Name of Treatment
fields.questionText: What was the [study treatment/investigational product] name?
fields.definition: Name of the study treatment or intervention given per single administration or during the constant dosing interval for the observation.

fields.name: EXREFID
fields.label: Exposure Reference ID
fields.questionText: What is the [study treatment/dose] label identifier?
fields.definition: An internal or external identifier (e.g., kit number, bottle label, vial identifier).

fields.name: EXLOT
fields.label: Lot Number
fields.questionText: What was the lot number of the[study treatment/dose] used?
fields.definition: Lot number of the EXTRT product.

fields.name: EXFAST
fields.label: Exposure Fasting Status
fields.questionText: Was the subject fasting?
fields.definition: An indication that the subject has abstained from food/water for the specified amount of time.

fields.name: EXDOSFRM
fields.label: Exposure Dose Form
fields.questionText: What was the dose form of the [study treatment/dose] ?
fields.definition: The dosage form in which the EXTRT is physically presented.

fields.name: EXSTDAT
fields.label: Exposure Start Date
fields.questionText: What was the ([intended/planned/actual]) ([study treatment/dose]) (start) date?
fields.definition: The start date of study treatment, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: EXSTTIM
fields.label: Exposure Start Time
fields.questionText: What was the ([intended/planned/actual]) ([study treatment/dose]) (start) time?
fields.definition: The start time of the study treatment, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: EXENDAT
fields.label: Exposure End Date
fields.questionText: What was the ([intended/planned/actual]) ([study treatment/dose]) (end) date?
fields.definition: The end date of study treatment, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: EXENTIM
fields.label: Exposure End Time
fields.questionText: What was the ([intended/planned/actual]) ([study treatment/dose]) (end) time?
fields.definition: The end time of treatment, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: EXDSTXT
fields.label: Exposure Dose Description
fields.questionText: What was the dose [per administration] (of [study treatment/dose]) ?
fields.definition: Dose (per administration).

fields.name: EXDOSU
fields.label: Exposure Dose Unit
fields.questionText: What was the unit for the dose?
fields.definition: The unit for intended dose (per administration) for EXDOSE, EXDOSTOT, or EXDOSTXT.

fields.name: EXDOSFRQ
fields.label: Exposure Dosing Frequency per Interval
fields.questionText: What was the frequency of[study treatment/dose] dosing?
fields.definition: The number of doses given/administered/taken during a specific interval.

fields.name: EXROUTE
fields.label: Exposure Route of Administration
fields.questionText: What was the route of administration (of the [study treatment/dose] )?
fields.definition: The route of administration of the study treatment.

fields.name: EXDOSRGM
fields.label: Intended Dose Regimen
fields.questionText: What was the intended dose regimen (of the [study treatment/dose] )?
fields.definition: The text description of the intended schedule or regimen for the Intervention.

fields.name: EXDOSADJ
fields.label: Dose Adjusted
fields.questionText: Was the dose adjusted?
fields.definition: An indication of whether the dose was adjusted.

fields.name: EXADJ
fields.label: Reason for Dose Adjustment
fields.questionText: What was the reason the dose was adjusted (from planned)?
fields.definition: Description of or explanation for why a dose of the study treatment was adjusted.

fields.name: EXITRPYN
fields.label: EX Exposure Interrupted
fields.questionText: Was the [ (study) treatment/dose] interrupted?
fields.definition: An indication of whether the exposure was interrupted.

fields.name: EXCINTD
fields.label: Exposure Interruption Duration
fields.questionText: If the dose was interrupted, how long was the interruption?
fields.definition: The collected duration of the treatment interruption.

fields.name: EXCINTDU
fields.label: Exposure Interruption Duration Units
fields.questionText: If the dose was interrupted, what were the units for the interruption duration?
fields.definition: The unit for the collected duration of treatment interruption.

fields.name: EXLOC
fields.label: Exposure Location of Dose Administration
fields.questionText: What was the anatomical location of the ([study treatment/dose] ) administration?
fields.definition: A description of the anatomical location of administration.

fields.name: EXVAMT
fields.label: Exposure Vehicle Amount
fields.questionText: What was the total amount (Drug + Vehicle)(of [study treatment/dose] ) administered?
fields.definition: The amount of the prepared product (treatment + vehicle) administered or given.

fields.name: EXVAMTU
fields.label: Exposure Vehicle Amount Units
fields.questionText: What was the unit for the amount (of [study treatment/dose] ) administered?
fields.definition: The unit of measure for the prepared product (treatment + vehicle).

fields.name: EXFLRT
fields.label: Exposure Infusion Rate
fields.questionText: What was the [study treatment/dose] infusion rate?
fields.definition: The flow rate for the total amount of drug + vehicle administered to the subject.

fields.name: EXFLRTU
fields.label: Exposure Infusion Rate Unit
fields.questionText: What were the units for the [study treatment/dose] infusion rate?
fields.definition: The unit of measure for the flow rate for the total amount of drug + vehicle administered to the subject.

fields.name: EXTPT
fields.label: Exposure Planned Time Point Name
fields.questionText: What was the planned time point for [study treatment/dose] ?
fields.definition: A text description of the planned time point when measurements should be taken, as defined in the protocol.

fields.name: EXTRTCMP
fields.label: Completed Treatment
fields.questionText: Did the subject complete the full course of [study treatment/dose] ?
fields.definition: Indication of whether the subject completed the intended regimen.

fields.name: EXLAT
fields.label: Exposure Laterality
fields.questionText: What was the side of the anatomical location of the ([study treatment/dose] ) administration?
fields.definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing side of the body for the study treatment administration.

fields.name: EXDIR
fields.label: Exposure Directionality
fields.questionText: What was the directionality of the anatomical location of the ([study treatment/dose] ) administration?
fields.definition: Qualifier further detailing the position of the anatomical location, relative to the center of the body, organ, or specimen.

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: CMCAT
fields.label: Category for Medication
fields.questionText: What is the category for the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy]?
fields.definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: CMSCAT
fields.label: Subcategory for Medication
fields.questionText: What is the subcategory for the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy]?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the CMCAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: CMYN
fields.label: Any Concomitant Medications Taken
fields.questionText: Were/Was any (concomitant) [medication(s)/treatment(s)/therapy(ies)] taken?
fields.definition: An indication of whether any (concomitant) medications/treatments/therapies were taken/given.

fields.name: CMSPID
fields.label: CM Sponsor-Defined Identifier
fields.questionText: [Sponsor-defined question]
fields.definition: A sponsor-defined identifier. In CDASH, this is typically used for preprinted or auto-generated numbers on the CRF, or any other type of identifier that does not already have a defined CDASH identifier field.

fields.name: CMTRT
fields.label: Reported Name of Drug, Med, or Therapy
fields.questionText: What was the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy] (name/term)?
fields.definition: Verbatim medication name or treatments (include only treatments with data collection characteristics similar to medications).

fields.name: CMPRESP
fields.label: CM Prespecified
fields.definition: An indication that a specific intervention or a group of interventions is prespecified on a CRF.

fields.name: CMOCCUR
fields.label: CM Occurrence
fields.questionText: Did the subject take [prespecified (concomitant) medication/treatment/therapy/dose]?; Has the subject taken [prespecified (concomitant) medication/treatment/therapy/dose]?
fields.definition: An indication whether the prespecified medication/treatment/therapy (CMTRT) or the group of medications/treatments/therapies was administered when information about the occurrence of a specific intervention was solicited.

fields.name: CMINGRD
fields.label: Concomitant Meds Active Ingredients
fields.questionText: What were the active ingredients?
fields.definition: Medication ingredients.

fields.name: CMINDC
fields.label: CM Indication
fields.questionText: For what indication was the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy] taken?
fields.definition: The condition, disease, symptom, or disorder that the concomitant (non-study) medication/treatment/therapy was used to address or investigate (e.g., why the medication/treatment/therapy was taken or administered).

fields.name: CMAENO
fields.label: Related Adverse Event ID
fields.questionText: What was the identifier for the adverse event(s) for which the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy] was taken?
fields.definition: Identifier for the adverse event that is the indication for this medication/treatment/therapy.

fields.name: CMMHNO
fields.label: Related Medical History Event ID
fields.questionText: What was the identifier for the medical history event(s) for which the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy] was taken?
fields.definition: Identifier for the medical history condition that is the indication for this medication/treatment/therapy.

fields.name: CMDOSE
fields.label: CM Dose per Administration
fields.questionText: What was the individual dose (of the concomitant [medication/treatment/therapy] per administration)?
fields.definition: The dose of medication/treatment (e.g., --TRT ) given at one time, represented as a numeric value.

fields.name: CMDSTXT
fields.label: Concomitant Meds Dose Description
fields.questionText: What was the individual dose of the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy]?
fields.definition: The dose of medication/treatment taken per administration.

fields.name: CMDOSTOT
fields.label: CM Total Daily Dose
fields.questionText: What was the total daily dose of the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy]?
fields.definition: The total amount of CMTRT taken over a day, using the units in CMDOSU.

fields.name: CMDOSU
fields.label: CM Dose Units
fields.questionText: What is the unit (for the dose of concomitant [medication/treatment/therapy])?
fields.definition: The unit associated with the concomitant medication/treatment/therapy taken (e.g., mg in "2 mg 3 times per day").

fields.name: CMDOSFRM
fields.label: CM Dose Form
fields.questionText: What was the dose form of the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy]?
fields.definition: The pharmaceutical dosage form in which the CMTRT is physically presented.

fields.name: CMDOSFRQ
fields.label: CM Dosing Frequency per Interval
fields.questionText: What was the frequency of the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy]?
fields.definition: The number of doses given/administered/taken during a specific interval.

fields.name: CMROUTE
fields.label: CM Route of Administration
fields.questionText: What was the route of administration of the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy]?
fields.definition: The route of administration of the concomitant medication/treatment/therapy.

fields.name: CMSTDAT
fields.label: Concomitant Meds Start Date
fields.questionText: What was the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy/dose] start date?
fields.definition: The start date is when the concomitant medication/treatment/therapy was first taken, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: CMSTTIM
fields.label: Concomitant Meds Start Time
fields.questionText: What was the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy/dose] start time?
fields.definition: The time the concomitant medication/treatment/therapy was started, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: CMPRIOR
fields.label: Prior Concomitant Meds
fields.questionText: Was the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy] given/taken prior to [CMSTTPT]?; Was the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy] given/taken prior to study start?
fields.definition: Indication the concomitant medication/treatment/therapy was given or taken prior to [CMSTTPT] or prior to the date in DM.RFSTDTC.

fields.name: CMONGO
fields.label: Ongoing Concomitant Meds
fields.prompt: Ongoing (as of [the study-specific time point or period])
fields.questionText: Was the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy] ongoing (as of [the study-specific time point or period])?
fields.definition: Indication the concomitant medication/treatment/therapy is ongoing when no end date is provided.

fields.name: CMENDAT
fields.label: Concomitant Meds End Date
fields.questionText: What was the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy/dose] end date?
fields.definition: The date that the subject ended/stopped taking the concomitant medication/treatment/therapy, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: CMENTIM
fields.label: Concomitant Meds End Time
fields.questionText: What was the [medication/treatment/therapy/dose] end time?
fields.definition: The time when the subject ended/stopped taking the concomitant medication/treatment/therapy, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: CMRSDISC
fields.label: Reason for Treatment Discontinuation
fields.prompt: Reason for discontinuation of concomitant medication/treatment
fields.questionText: What was the reason the (concomitant) [medication/treatment/therapy/ --TRT] was [discontinued/stopped/ended]?
fields.definition: The reason the treatment was discontinued.

fields.name: CMDECOD
fields.label: Standardized Medication Name
fields.definition: The dictionary or sponsor-defined standardized text description of the topic variable, CMTRT, or the modified topic variable (CMMODIFY), if applicable.

fields.name: CMCLAS
fields.label: CM Medication Class
fields.definition: The class for the intervention (often obtained from a coding dictionary).

fields.name: CMCLASCD
fields.label: CM Medication Class Code
fields.definition: The assigned dictionary code for the class for the intervention.

fields.name: CMATC1
fields.label: ATC Level 1 Description
fields.definition: Dictionary text description of the first level of hierarchy within the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification system; indicates the anatomical main group.

fields.name: CMATC1CD
fields.label: ATC Level 1 Code
fields.definition: Dictionary code denoting the first level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the anatomical main group.

fields.name: CMATC2
fields.label: ATC Level 2 Description
fields.definition: Dictionary text description for the second level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the therapeutic main group.

fields.name: CMATC2CD
fields.label: ATC Level 2 Code
fields.definition: Dictionary code denoting the second level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the therapeutic main group.

fields.name: CMATC3
fields.label: ATC Level 3 Description
fields.definition: Dictionary text description of the third level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the therapeutic/pharmacological subgroup.

fields.name: CMATC3CD
fields.label: ATC Level 3 Code
fields.definition: Dictionary code denoting the third level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the therapeutic/pharmacological subgroup.

fields.name: CMATC4
fields.label: ATC Level 4 Description
fields.definition: Dictionary text description of the fourth level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the chemical/therapeutic/pharmacological subgroup.

fields.name: CMATC4CD
fields.label: ATC Level 4 Code
fields.definition: Dictionary code denoting the fourth level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the chemical/therapeutic/pharmacological subgroup.

fields.name: CMATC5
fields.label: ATC Level 5 Description
fields.definition: Dictionary text description of the fifth level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the chemical substance.

fields.name: CMATC5CD
fields.label: ATC Level 5 Code
fields.definition: Dictionary code denoting the fifth level of hierarchy within the ATC classification system; indicates the chemical substance.

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: AEYN
fields.label: Any Adverse Event
fields.questionText: Were any adverse events experienced?
fields.definition: An indication of whether any AEs were experienced during the study.

fields.name: AECAT
fields.label: Category for Adverse Event
fields.questionText: What is the category of the adverse event?
fields.definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: AESCAT
fields.label: Subcategory for Adverse Event
fields.questionText: What is the subcategory of the adverse event?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the AECAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: AESPID
fields.label: AE Sponsor-Defined Identifier
fields.questionText: [Sponsor-defined question]
fields.definition: A sponsor-defined identifier. In CDASH, This is typically used for preprinted or auto-generated numbers on the CRF, or any other type of identifier that does not already have a defined CDASH identifier field.

fields.name: AETERM
fields.label: Reported Term for the Adverse Event
fields.questionText: What is the adverse event term?
fields.definition: The reported or prespecified name of the adverse event.

fields.name: AEOCCUR
fields.label: Adverse Event Occurrence
fields.questionText: Did the subject have [prespecified adverse event/group of adverse events]?
fields.definition: An indication of whether a prespecified adverse event or a group of adverse events occurred when information about the occurrence of a specific event is solicited.

fields.name: AEPRESP
fields.label: prespecified Adverse Event
fields.definition: An indication that a specific event or group of events are prespecified on a CRF.

fields.name: AESTDAT
fields.label: Adverse Event Start Date
fields.questionText: What is the adverse event start date?
fields.definition: The start date of the adverse event, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: AESTTIM
fields.label: Start Time of Adverse Event
fields.questionText: What is the adverse event start time?
fields.definition: The start time of the adverse event, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: AELOC
fields.label: AE Location of Event
fields.questionText: What is the anatomical location of the adverse event?
fields.definition: A description of the anatomical location relevant for the adverse event.

fields.name: AELAT
fields.label: Adverse Event Laterality
fields.questionText: What is the side of the anatomical location of the adverse event?
fields.definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing the side of the body relevant for the event.

fields.name: AEDIR
fields.label: Adverse Event Directionality
fields.questionText: What is the directionality of the anatomical location of the adverse event?
fields.definition: Qualifier further detailing the position of the anatomical location, relative to the center of the body, organ, or specimen.

fields.name: AEPORTOT
fields.label: AE Location Portion or Totality
fields.questionText: What is the portion or totality of the anatomical location of the adverse event?
fields.definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing the distribution (i.e., arrangement of, apportioning of).

fields.name: AEONGO
fields.label: Ongoing Adverse Event
fields.prompt: Ongoing (as of [the study-specific time point or period])
fields.questionText: Is the adverse event ongoing (as of [the study-specific time point or period])?
fields.definition: Indication that an adverse event is ongoing when no end date is provided.

fields.name: AEENDAT
fields.label: Adverse Event End Date
fields.questionText: What was the adverse event end date?
fields.definition: The date when the adverse event resolved/ended, represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: AEENTIM
fields.label: End Time of Adverse Event
fields.questionText: What was the adverse event end time?
fields.definition: The time when the adverse event ended/resolved, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: AESEV
fields.label: AE Severity/Intensity
fields.questionText: What is the severity of the adverse event?
fields.definition: The severity or intensity of the event.

fields.name: AETOXGR
fields.label: AE Standard Toxicity Grade
fields.prompt: [NCI CTCAE/ Name of the scale] (Toxicity) Grade
fields.questionText: What is the [NCI CTCAE/Name of scale (toxicity) grade] of the adverse event?
fields.definition: The grade of the severity of the event using a standard "toxicity" scale (e.g., NCI CTCAE).

fields.name: AESER
fields.label: AE Serious Event
fields.questionText: Was the adverse event serious?
fields.definition: An indication of whether the adverse event is determined to be "serious," based on what is defined in the regulations/protocol.

fields.name: AESDTH
fields.label: Results in Death
fields.questionText: Did the adverse event result in death?
fields.definition: An indication the serious adverse event resulted in death.

fields.name: DTHDAT
fields.label: Death Date
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the subject's date of death?
fields.definition: Date of death for any subject who died.

fields.name: AESLIFE
fields.label: Is Life Threatening
fields.questionText: Was the adverse event life threatening?
fields.definition: An indication the serious adverse event was life threatening.

fields.name: AESHOSP
fields.label: Requires or Prolongs Hospitalization
fields.questionText: Did the adverse event result in initial or prolonged hospitalization for the subject?
fields.definition: An indication the serious adverse event resulted in an initial or prolonged hospitalization.

fields.name: AESDISAB
fields.label: Persist or Signif Disability/Incapacity
fields.questionText: Did the adverse event result in disability or permanent damage?
fields.definition: An indication the serious adverse event was associated with a persistent or significant disability or incapacity.

fields.name: AESCONG
fields.label: Congenital Anomaly or Birth Defect
fields.questionText: Was the adverse event associated with a congenital anomaly or birth defect?
fields.definition: An indication the serious adverse event was associated with a congenital anomaly or birth defect.

fields.name: AESINTV
fields.label: Needs Intervention to Prevent Impairment
fields.questionText: Did the adverse event require intervention to prevent permanent impairment or damage resulting from the use of a medical product?
fields.definition: An indication an adverse event required medical or surgical intervention to preclude permanent impairment of a body function, or prevent permanent damage to a body structure, due to the use of a medical product.

fields.name: AESMIE
fields.label: Other Medically Important Serious Event
fields.questionText: Was the adverse event a medically important event not covered by other serious criteria?
fields.definition: An indication additional categories for seriousness apply.

fields.name: AESCAN
fields.label: Involves Cancer
fields.questionText: Was the adverse event associated with the development of cancer?
fields.definition: An indication the serious event was associated with the development of cancer.

fields.name: AESOD
fields.label: Occurred with Overdose
fields.questionText: Did the adverse event occur with an overdose?
fields.definition: An indication the serious event occurred with an overdose.

fields.name: AEREL
fields.label: AE Causality
fields.questionText: Was this adverse event related to study treatment?
fields.definition: An indication the study treatment had a causal effect on the adverse event, as determined by the clinician/investigator.

fields.name: AEACN
fields.label: Action Taken with Study Treatment
fields.questionText: What action was taken with study treatment?
fields.definition: A description of the action taken with study treatment as a result of the event.

fields.name: AEACNDEV
fields.label: Actions Taken with Device
fields.questionText: What action was taken with a device used in the study?
fields.definition: A description of the action taken, with respect to a device used in a study (which may or may not be the device under study), as a result of the event.

fields.name: AEACNOYN
fields.label: Any Other Actions Taken
fields.questionText: Were any other actions taken in response to this adverse event?
fields.definition: An indication whether any other actions were taken in response to the adverse event that were unrelated to study treatment dose changes or other non-study treatments given because of this adverse event.

fields.name: AEACNOTH
fields.label: Other Action Taken
fields.questionText: What other action was taken?
fields.definition: A description of other action taken as a result of the event that is unrelated to dose adjustments of the study treatment.

fields.name: AEOUT
fields.label: Outcome of Adverse Event
fields.questionText: What is the outcome of this adverse event?
fields.definition: A description of the outcome of an event.

fields.name: AEDIS
fields.label: AE Caused Study Discontinuation
fields.questionText: Did the adverse event cause the subject to be discontinued from the study?
fields.definition: An indication of whether the event caused the subject to discontinue from the study.

fields.name: AERLNSYN
fields.label: AE Related to Non-Study Treatment
fields.questionText: Was this adverse event due to treatment other than study treatment?
fields.definition: An indication whether, in the investigator's opinion, the event may have been due to a treatment other than study treatment.

fields.name: AERELNST
fields.label: AE Relationship to Non-Study Treatment
fields.questionText: What is the relationship to non-study treatment?
fields.definition: Description of the investigator's opinion as to whether the adverse event may have been due to a treatment other than study treatment.

fields.name: AESI
fields.label: Adverse Event of Special Interest
fields.questionText: Is this event of special interest?
fields.definition: An adverse event of special interest (serious or non-serious) is one of scientific and medical concern specific to the sponsor's product or program, for which ongoing monitoring and rapid communication by the investigator to the sponsor can be appropriate. Such an event might warrant further investi

fields.name: AEPATT
fields.label: Pattern of Adverse Event
fields.questionText: What is the adverse event pattern?
fields.definition: Used to indicate the pattern of the event over time.

fields.name: AECONTRT
fields.label: Concomitant or Additional Trtmnt Given
fields.prompt: Concomitant or Additional Treatment Given Due to This AE
fields.questionText: Was a concomitant or additional treatment given due to this adverse event?
fields.definition: An indication whether a concomitant or additional treatment given because of the occurrence of the event.

fields.name: AEMODIFY
fields.label: AE Modified Reported Term
fields.definition: If the value for AETERM is modified to facilitate coding, then AEMODIFY will contain the modified text.

fields.name: AEDECOD
fields.label: AE Dictionary-Derived Term
fields.definition: The dictionary or standardized text description of AETERM or the modified topic variable (AEMODIFY), if applicable.

fields.name: AELLT
fields.label: AE Lowest Level Term
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the lowest level term.

fields.name: AELLTCD
fields.label: AE Lowest Level Term Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the lowest level term.

fields.name: AEPTCD
fields.label: AE Preferred Term Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the preferred term.

fields.name: AEHLT
fields.label: AE High Level Term
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the high level term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: AEHLTCD
fields.label: AE High Level Term Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the high level term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: AEHLGT
fields.label: AE High Level Group Term
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the high level group term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: AEHLGTCD
fields.label: AE High Level Group Term Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the high level group term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: AESOC
fields.label: AE Primary System Organ Class
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: AESOCCD
fields.label: AE Primary System Organ Class Code
fields.definition: The dictionary-derived code for the primary system organ class (SOC).

fields.name: STUDYID
fields.label: Study Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the study identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a study.

fields.name: SITEID
fields.label: Study Site Identifier
fields.questionText: What is the site identifier?
fields.definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.

fields.name: SUBJID
fields.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
fields.questionText: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
fields.definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.

fields.name: DSCAT
fields.label: Category for Disposition Event
fields.questionText: What was the category of the disposition?
fields.definition: A categorization of the disposition events, which is used to distinguish between disposition events, protocol milestones, and other events.

fields.name: DSSCAT
fields.label: Subcategory for Disposition Event
fields.questionText: What was the subcategory of the disposition?
fields.definition: A sub-division of the DSCAT values based on user-defined characteristics.

fields.name: EPOCH
fields.label: Epoch
fields.questionText: What is the trial period for this disposition event?
fields.definition: Trial Epoch (e.g., 'BLINDED TREATMENT', 'OPEN LABEL TREATMENT', 'SCREENING', 'RUN-IN') for which subject disposition is being collected.

fields.name: DSDECOD
fields.label: Standardized Disposition Term
fields.questionText: [Sponsor-defined]
fields.definition: The standardized terminology of the disposition term.

fields.name: DSTERM
fields.label: Reported Term for the Disposition Event
fields.questionText: [Sponsor-defined]
fields.definition: The verbatim or prespecified name of the disposition term.

fields.name: DSSTDAT
fields.label: Disposition Event Start Date
fields.questionText: What was the [protocol milestone/other event name] (start) date?
fields.definition: The date of the specified protocol milestone (e.g., informed consent, randomization) represented in an unambiguous date format (e.g., DD-MON-YYYY).

fields.name: DSSTTIM
fields.label: Disposition Event Start Time
fields.questionText: What was the [protocol milestone/other event name] (start) time?
fields.definition: The time of the specified protocol milestone (e.g., informed consent, randomization) or other event, represented in an unambiguous time format (e.g., hh:mm:ss).

fields.name: DSUNBLND
fields.label: Unblinded
fields.questionText: Was (study) treatment unblinded by the site?
fields.definition: An indication of whether the subject's treatment information was revealed to any unauthorized site personnel during the trial.
